subject: Product Management
title: Technology Adoption in Enterprise
description: A fast breakdown of Technology Adoption in Enterprise for builders.

hook.text: Enterprise software adoption is painfully slow. A company with 10,000 employees can take 18 months to adopt a new tool.

buildup.text: Enterprise adoption involves procurement, security review, IT integration, training, change management, and executive sign-off.

discovery.text: Bottom-up adoption changed this: tools like Slack and Notion spread team by team inside companies before IT even knew about them.

twist.text: Bottom-up tools eventually hit a wall: IT demands SSO, compliance, and admin controls.

climax.text: The winning playbook: land with individuals and teams (product-led), then expand with enterprise features (sales-led).

punchline.text: Start with users. Win their hearts. Then sell to their bosses.

quiz.question: What is bottom-up enterprise adoption?
options: Tools spread team by team inside companies before IT or procurement gets involved
A top-down mandate from the CEO to use a specific tool
Enterprise adoption that starts with the IT department
